items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. Your retirement plan might disagree.
items.description: More investors want their money to reflect their values. It feels righteous — but the reality is a lot messier.

items.title: If the stock market’s double bubble bursts, it could usher in the next crash
items.description: While valuations still look extreme relative to history, the recent pace of corporate earnings growth has also meaningfully diverged from the long-term trend.

items.title: Your 5 biggest ‘Trump account’ questions, answered
items.description: Here’s who is eligible for $1,000 in free money — and how to sign up.

items.title: Micron’s stock gains, signaling a ‘return to optimism’ about the chip sector
items.description: Investors are looking forward to Samsung’s earnings and SK Hynix’s ADR listing, experts say.

items.title: TeraWulf’s stock gains after a $19 billion deal with Anthropic
items.description: The deal “validates” the crypto-mining company’s pivot to supporting the AI buildout, its CEO said.

items.title: Strategy is losing a lot of money on bitcoin. Here’s why it’s selling anyway.
items.description: Strategy sold more than 3,000 bitcoins to raise cash to pay dividends on its preferred stock — something Executive Chair Michael Saylor had previously said it didn’t need to do.

items.title: Dell’s stock gets another Trump bump — but this time it’s fading
items.description: The president recommended that people buy Dell computers while speaking about the launch of “Trump accounts” on Monday.

items.title: How parents can take advantage of the ‘best summer-camp tax break in years’
items.description: For many kids, summer means camp. For many of their parents, that means financial juggling to pay for those camp experiences.

items.title: Silver has plunged 50% from its January peak to $60. Why it could hit $130 next year.
items.description: After its dramatic plunge, silver may be experiencing a huge disconnect between its traded value and its importance as a critical material.

items.title: Who should handle your estate plan: Your lawyer or your financial adviser?
items.description: The more people who have eyeballs on your situation, the better — but at some point, you are going to need a legal specialist.
